title: Avoid Throwing Checked Exceptions in Java
description: Checked exceptions should not be thrown as they can negatively impact the readability of source code by spreading error handling/propagation logic everywhere. Instead, consider using unchecked exceptions or other error handling mechanisms.
recommendation: Avoid throwing new checked exceptions in your methods. Consider using unchecked exceptions or other error handling mechanisms.
impact: Improves code readability by reducing the spread of error handling/propagation logic in the code.

checks.message: Avoid throwing new checked exceptions in your methods. Consider using unchecked exceptions or other error handling mechanisms.
